sentence: Subjective:

HPI: The patient, a 38-year-old male, presented to the office with a complaint of right knee pain that began a week ago after an incident at a trampoline park where he heard a snap and experienced immediate pain.
He reports that the pain is located on the outside of the knee and has not improved with over-the-counter pain medication or home remedies such as icing and elevating the knee.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension, for which he is currently taking 20mg of lisinopril daily.
He also has diabetes, for which he is taking metformin 500mg daily.
He has not been consistently checking his blood sugar levels, aiming to do so 4-5 times a week.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure was 180/80.
Physical exam: On physical examination, the patient had a slight limp and a noticeable bruising and swelling (ecchymosis and edema) along the lateral aspect of the knee.
The patient was able to bend and straighten his knee, but experienced pain on the outside of the knee with palpation.
Test Results: The patient's right knee x-ray showed no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a lateral ligament strain of the right knee, hypertension, and diabetes.
Problem list: Lateral ligament strain of the right knee; Hypertension; Diabetes.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg daily for pain and swelling associated with the lateral ligament strain of the right knee.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to help strengthen the muscles around the area and prevent further injury.
I will continue the patient's current lisinopril regimen at 20mg daily and order an echocardiogram to evaluate the patient's cardiac function.
I also ordered an A1C to assess the patient's long-term blood sugar control and a lipid panel to evaluate the patient's lipid profile.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a follow-up appointment to discuss the results of the imaging and laboratory tests.
